change_reason: Child 4 (WI-4324) Ollama Phase 1: insert GOV-HARNESS-ONBOARDING-CONTRACT-001 per GO -002; content derived from bridge/gtkb-ollama-integration-phase-1-001.md drafts + -003 guard-adapter delta; owner anchor DELIB-20260663.

As interactive Prime I mint packets with presented_to_user=true and transcript_captured=true by presenting full artifact content in chat. full_content:
{
  "id": "GOV-HARNESS-ONBOARDING-CONTRACT-001",
  "type": "governance",
  "status": "specified",
  "title": "Harness Onboarding Contract -- required artifacts, machine-checkable assertions, and capability floor for any new GT-KB coding harness",
  "scope": "GT-KB platform; any new AI coding harness (E, F, ...). First concrete adoption is Ollama (harness D) per ADR-OLLAMA-HARNESS-ADOPTION-001.",
  "tags": ["governance", "harness-onboarding", "capability-floor", "ollama", "phase-1"],
  "description": "Contract: Any new AI coding harness (E, F, ...) MUST satisfy three layers.

Layer 1 -- Required Artifacts (procedural):
1. Entry in harness-state/harness-identities.json with a unique installation-stable id.
2. Row in harness-state/harness-registry.json with a canonical role field (JSON list: [], ["prime-builder"], ["loyal-opposition"], or both) and a status field (active, registered, suspended).
3. Entry in scripts/check_harness_parity.py::KNOWN_HARNESSES.
4. A [harnesses.<harness>] capability block in config/agent-control/harness-capability-registry.toml (the capability floor; see Layer 3). (Historical note: earlier drafts referenced [capabilities.<harness>]; the live location is [harnesses.<harness>] per the foundation child cross-child dependency.)
5. Glossary entry in .claude/rules/canonical-terminology.md per DCL-CONCEPT-ON-CONTACT-001.
6. Entry in .claude/rules/operating-model.md section 3 (implemented or intended).
7. An ADR (e.g., ADR-<HARNESS>-HARNESS-ADOPTION-NNN).
8. Doctor _check_<harness>_harness function in groundtruth-kb/src/groundtruth_kb/project/doctor.py.

Layer 2 -- Machine-Checkable Assertions: for each <harness> in harness-identities.json: (1) a registry row with matching id, (2) a KNOWN_HARNESSES entry, (3) a capability block in the registry TOML, (4) a glossary entry (case-insensitive), (5) a doctor check function.

Layer 3 -- Capability Floor: each [harnesses.<harness>] block MUST declare:
1. bridge_compliance_gate_respect = true
2. root_boundary_respect = true
3. author_metadata_env_var_setting = true
4. destructive_gate_delegation = true
5. advertised_tool_subset subset of ("Read","Write","Edit","Grep","Glob","Bash")
6. tool_guard_adapter_fail_closed = true (per bridge/gtkb-ollama-integration-phase-1-003.md).

Layer 3 additional requirement: a harness that executes local mutating tools outside Claude Code or Codex hook runtimes MUST provide a fail-closed guard adapter that invokes the canonical GT-KB guard scripts before mutation. Declaring bridge_compliance_gate_respect = true is not sufficient unless that adapter is implemented and tested.

Severity: Layer 1 procedural-only; Layer 2 blocking; Layer 3 blocking.

Related: ADR-OLLAMA-HARNESS-ADOPTION-001 (first concrete adoption), GOV-HARNESS-ROLE-PORTABILITY-001, GOV-SESSION-ROLE-AUTHORITY-001, ADR-CODEX-HOOK-PARITY-FALLBACK-001. Owner decision anchor: DELIB-20260663 AUQ#7 (heavy governance), AUQ#11 (procedural plus machine-checkable GOV reach).",
  "assertions": [
    {"type": "grep", "file": "config/agent-control/harness-capability-registry.toml", "pattern": "bridge_compliance_gate_respect = true", "description": "Capability floor: bridge-compliance-gate respect declared."},
    {"type": "grep", "file": "config/agent-control/harness-capability-registry.toml", "pattern": "root_boundary_respect = true", "description": "Capability floor: root-boundary respect declared."},
    {"type": "grep", "file": "config/agent-control/harness-capability-registry.toml", "pattern": "tool_guard_adapter_fail_closed = true", "description": "Capability floor: fail-closed guard adapter declared."},
    {"type": "grep", "file": "scripts/check_harness_parity.py", "pattern": "KNOWN_HARNESSES", "description": "Parity checker enumerates KNOWN_HARNESSES (Layer 1 item 3)."},
    {"kind": "behavioral", "description": "Layer 1 procedural artifacts (identity, registry, parity entry, capability block, glossary entry, operating-model section 3 entry, ADR, doctor check) all exist for the onboarded harness.", "verifying_test": "platform_tests/scripts/test_ollama_governance_artifacts.py + doctor _check_ollama_harness (Child 3 verification, bridge/gtkb-ollama-integration-phase-1-verification-012.md VERIFIED)."}
  ]
}
